city_personality.primary_industry_workers: minibus taxi drivers and informal traders
city_personality.slang_reaction: Eish, this government is a joke, chommie.

opening_sequence.title: Welcome to Egoli, Mayor
opening_sequence.intro: The City of Gold is rusting. Load shedding plunges the streets into darkness, the infrastructure is collapsing, and the gap between Sandton and Soweto has never been wider.

advisors.name: Thabo Mofokeng
advisors.hidden_agenda: Awarding lucrative municipal contracts to his cousin's shell companies.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Implementing a strict, open-source tender review process.

briefing: Revenue collection in the townships is practically zero.
We have a massive shortfall this quarter.
The tender for the new billing system is ready for your signature.
disagreement: You are leaving money on the table.
This will upset very important people.
dialogue.betrayal: You closed the tap. I'm joining the opposition and taking my donors with me.
roast: You run this city like a spaza shop.
Even the pigeons manage their crumbs better than you.

advisors.name: Captain Hendrik Van Der Merwe
advisors.hidden_agenda: Privatizing security in affluent suburbs to benefit his former colleagues.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Banning private security companies from operating on public roads.

briefing: Cable theft has left the inner city dark again.
Illegal mining operations are destabilizing the roads.
Crime stats in the CBD are through the roof.
disagreement: The criminals are laughing at us.
dialogue.betrayal: You prefer chaos to order. I'm resigning to run a private firm that actually works.
roast: You are softer than a marshamallow.
This city is a crime scene and you are the suspect.
scandal_reaction: Keep the sirens loud to distract them.

advisors.name: Lerato Ndlovu
advisors.hidden_agenda: Expropriating abandoned inner-city buildings for social housing.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Selling off inner-city hijacked buildings to private developers instead of converting them.

briefing: The housing backlog is over 300,000 units.
Informal settlements are expanding along the highways.
We need to reclaim the hijacked buildings in Hillbrow.
disagreement: This is just apartheid spatial planning with a new coat of paint.
dialogue.betrayal: You sold out the working class to the developers. I'll see you in the streets.
roast: You build mansions while the people live in shacks.
Your vision for the city is completely blind.
scandal_reaction: I need to distance myself from this.

advisors.name: Sipho 'Vusi' Zungu
advisors.hidden_agenda: Ensuring the municipal bus service (Rea Vaya) never outcompetes the minibus taxis.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Massively expanding the Rea Vaya routes into heavily controlled taxi territory.

briefing: The taxi bosses are threatening a strike over petrol prices.
Potholes on the M1 are destroying suspensions.
Traffic lights are out across Sandton due to load shedding.
disagreement: You are poking the bear, Mayor.
dialogue.betrayal: You crossed the taxi bosses. Good luck governing a city that can't move.
roast: You are driving this city into a ditch.
Your policies have more potholes than our roads.

advisors.name: Bishop Desmond Khumalo
advisors.hidden_agenda: Securing a massive municipal grant for his mega-church's 'community outreach' program.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Cutting all funding to faith-based organizations to balance the budget.

briefing: The community is losing hope, Mayor.
Poverty is driving our youth to nyaope (drugs).
agreement: The congregation will pray for you.
disagreement: The people will suffer for this.
dialogue.betrayal: You have turned your back on the faithful. My sermons will reflect this.
roast: Even prayer cannot save your approval ratings.

decisions.title: The Power Crisis
decisions.body: The national grid is failing, causing daily blackouts (load shedding). The city must find a way to keep the lights on.

options.label: Purchase expensive diesel to run emergency generators for critical infrastructure.

consequences.public_reaction: At least the traffic lights are working.

options.label: Sign a massive contract with independent power producers (IPPs).

consequences.public_reaction: Finally, we are ditching the national utility.

options.label: Cut off power entirely to non-paying townships to stabilize the grid.

consequences.public_reaction: This means war! We will burn the substations!

decisions.title: The Hillbrow Hijackings
decisions.body: Criminal syndicates have taken over dozens of inner-city apartment buildings, collecting rent illegally while the buildings decay into slums.

options.label: Cut off water and electricity to force them out.

consequences.public_reaction: Cruel. Innocent people are suffering in there too.

options.label: Launch massive JMPD raids to physically evict the syndicates.
Offer amnesty and hand the title deeds over to the syndicates to 'formalize' them.

consequences.public_reaction: We just rewarded criminals with property!

decisions.title: The Copper Syndicate
decisions.body: Heavily armed syndicates are stealing kilometers of copper cable, leaving entire suburbs without power or internet.

options.label: Hire private security to guard major substations.

consequences.public_reaction: Expensive, but we need our internet back.

options.label: Ban all scrap metal yards in the city to destroy the market.

consequences.public_reaction: Legit businesses are paying the price.

options.label: Electrify the exposed cables with lethal voltage to deter thieves.

consequences.public_reaction: People are dying in the streets!

decisions.title: The Pothole Epidemic
decisions.body: Heavy rains and neglected maintenance have turned the roads into an obstacle course, damaging thousands of vehicles.

options.label: Launch a slow, internal municipal patching program.

consequences.public_reaction: Too little, too late. They wash away in the next rain.

options.label: Privatize road maintenance to a major corporate sponsor.

consequences.public_reaction: Brought to you by Discovery Health. Whatever works.

options.label: Tell citizens to fix the potholes themselves and deduct it from their rates.

consequences.public_reaction: What are we paying taxes for?!

decisions.title: Taxi Route Wars
decisions.body: Rival minibus taxi associations are shooting at each other over lucrative routes, catching commuters in the crossfire.

options.label: Mediate a peace deal and grant both sides more routes.
Deploy the military to shut down the contested taxi ranks entirely.

consequences.public_reaction: How do we get to work now?!

options.label: Subsidize the heavily armed private security of one side to crush the other.

consequences.public_reaction: The city is officially funding gang warfare.

crises.name: Outbreak of Xenophobic Violence
crises.trigger: Tensions over unemployment have boiled over into violent attacks on foreign-owned shops.
crises.description: Mobs are looting and burning stores in the CBD and Alexandra. Foreign nationals are fleeing, and the international community is threatening sanctions. The police are vastly outnumbered.

options.label: Condemn the violence and set up safe camps for the displaced.
Order the police to use live ammunition to stop the looters.
Pandering to the mob, promise mass deportations of undocumented immigrants.

crises.name: Rand Water Reservoir Collapse
crises.trigger: Total failure of the pumping stations has left 80% of the city without running water.
crises.description: Hospitals are cancelling surgeries. Schools are closed. Citizens are queuing for hours at erratic water tankers, and desperation is leading to clashes in the streets.

options.label: Deploy hundreds of emergency water tankers at massive cost.
Implement strict water rationing: affluent areas cut to supply the hospitals.
Blame the national utility and urge citizens to collect rainwater.

crises.name: Massive Municipal Strike
crises.trigger: SAMWU (the municipal workers union) has gone on strike, bringing the city to a halt.
crises.description: Trash is piling up in the streets, buses aren't running, and licensing departments are closed. The union demands a 15% wage increase that will bankrupt the city.

options.label: Cave to the demands and take on massive debt to pay them.
Refuse the demands and fire striking workers, hiring private contractors.
Deploy the police to violently break up the union blockades.

crises.name: Coalition Government Collapse
crises.trigger: Your fragile political coalition has shattered, and a motion of no confidence is looming.
crises.description: Small kingmaker parties are demanding impossible concessions to keep you in power. If you fail the vote tomorrow, you are ousted as Mayor instantly.

options.label: Hand over key portfolios to the extortionate kingmaker parties.
Call their bluff, face the vote, and rely on public pressure.
Bribe opposition councilors with massive cash payouts to cross the floor.

scandals.title: Massive PPE Tender Fraud Exposed
scandals.description: Journalists revealed that millions in municipal contracts were awarded to companies owned by your immediate family.

scandals.title: Mayor Caught Fleeing Accident Scene
scandals.description: You crashed your official vehicle into a streetlight after a party and fled before the metro police arrived.

scandals.title: State Capture Allegations Hit City Hall
scandals.description: Leaked emails show wealthy private businessmen dictating policy and cabinet appointments directly to your office.

positive: Credit to the Mayor, this actually works.
Let's hope they keep this momentum.
Potholes fixed in my area today, shocking!
Good job, just don't steal the money.
neutral: We'll see if it lasts past the elections.
Eish, I don't trust these politicians.
Whatever, just keep the lights on.
Let's wait for the tender corruption to be exposed.
negative: We are tired of voting for thieves.
The city is collapsing and they do this?
Load shedding is killing my business.
media: The Star: Controversy surrounds the Mayor's latest directive.
News24: Opposition demands answers over city budget.
eNCA: Traffic at a standstill due to protests.
Daily Maverick: The rot in Joburg's administration deepens.
Radio 702: Callers express fury over continued power cuts.
City Press: Is this the end of the coalition?
politician: We will not stand for this corruption!
A necessary step for our fiscal recovery.
The ANC/DA/EFF must be held accountable.
This is an attack on the poor.
We urge patience while we rebuild.
The previous administration left us a mess.
activist: Housing is a right, not a privilege!
street: Chommie, the taxis are striking again, I'm stuck.
Stage 6 load shedding tonight, fetch the candles.
Eish, my tires are gone because of these potholes.
They only care about Sandton, bru.
Cable theft in my area, no power for 3 days.
crisis: We have no water, we are desperate.

scandal_reactions.minor: Eish, just another tenderpreneur making money.
scandal_reactions.moderate: Disgusting, but not surprising in this country.
scandal_reactions.major: We will march to the Mayor's office tomorrow!
scandal_reactions.career_ending: You are going to prison. Finished and klaar.

romance_exposure.flavour: The tabloids love it, and there are jokes on Twitter, but it's quickly forgotten in the face of actual service delivery failures.
